description: Stage 3.1: Cross-field mechanism transfer. Lane B (export-only) patches test on existing checkpoint. Lane A (training) patches require full training runs. Each hypothesis imports a structural concept from a non-ML field.

sanity.description: Crash check for Lane A (training) slots only. Lane B uses lane_b_bakeoff.

screen.description: Short training screen for Lane A candidates. Ranked against Lane A controls only.

lane_b_bakeoff.description: Lane B export-only: load control checkpoint, apply export patch, quantize, eval. No training. Ranked against Lane B control (same checkpoint, no patch).

composite.description: Best Lane A winner + best Lane B winner composed. Also best train-process + best export child.

final_single.description: Alias for decision phase. Kept for backward compatibility.

decision.description: Full-length runs for promoted finalists from both lanes.

slots.name: baseline_control_a
slots.why: Primary control: 1.1233 frontier code, unpatched. Reproduces the known-best result.
slots.validates: The base code is correct and the patching pipeline doesn't break it.
slots.falsifies: All derivative results are suspect if this doesn't match expected range.
notes: Expected post-quant BPB at 180s: ~1.18-1.20 range (1-GPU short run).

slots.name: baseline_control_b
slots.why: Noise floor calibration. R0A vs R0B spread sets the kill threshold.
slots.validates: Short-run variance is small enough for meaningful pruning.
slots.falsifies: Screen is too noisy to separate signal from noise.
notes: Different seed from R0A to measure inter-seed variance.

slots.name: baseline_export_control
slots.why: Lane B control: same checkpoint as candidates, no export patch. All Lane B candidates must beat this to prove their export modification helps.
slots.validates: The export-only pipeline reproduces the control checkpoint's deployed score.
slots.falsifies: Lane B comparisons are invalid if this control diverges from expected range.
notes: Export-only: loads R0A checkpoint, runs standard export pipeline, no patches.

slots.name: companding_mu100
slots.broken_invariant: One quantization bin spacing for all weight magnitudes. Uniform int6 allocates equal bin width across the whole range.
slots.mechanism: Replace uniform int6 quantization with mu-law companding: compress(w) before quantize, expand(w) after dequantize. Concentrates bins near zero where weight density is highest.
slots.why: Weight distributions are heavy-tailed. Uniform int6 wastes resolution on sparse tails while under-resolving the dense near-zero region. mu-law companding from telecom (ITU-T G.711) directly addresses this.
slots.early_signal: quant_damage (post_quant_bpb - pre_quant_bpb) should shrink vs R0A_export control.
slots.failure_mode: mu=100 is too aggressive, distorting tails. Or percentile clipping in the original quantizer already adapts well enough.
slots.kill_rule: No improvement in post_quant_bpb vs R0A_export at lane_b_bakeoff.
slots.validates: Nonlinear requantization reduces quant damage. Opens the quantization function itself as an attack surface.
slots.falsifies: The percentile search in int6 quantization already adapts well enough. Linear binning with good clipping is near-optimal.
notes: Lane B: can be tested on existing checkpoint without retraining.
mu=100 is between telephone standard (255) and mild (50). If too strong, try mu=50.

slots.name: fisher_bit_allocation
slots.broken_invariant: One bit-width for all layers. Uniform int6 treats every layer as equally sensitive to quantization.
slots.mechanism: Compute diagonal Fisher approximation (E[grad^2]) per layer from 3 forward-backward passes. Allocate int8 to top-K highest-Fisher layers, int4 to bottom-K, int6 to rest. Budget-neutral: int4 savings offset int8 cost.
slots.why: Marginal loss increase per quantization error is not uniform across layers. High-Fisher layers (near output) are far more loss-sensitive. Uniform int6 wastes bits on insensitive early layers.
slots.early_signal: quant_damage should shrink vs R0A_export. Artifact size should stay within budget (int4 savings offset int8).
slots.failure_mode: All layers are roughly equally sensitive. Or int4 on low-Fisher layers causes disproportionate damage. Or Fisher estimate from 3 batches is too noisy.
slots.kill_rule: No improvement in post_quant_bpb vs R0A_export, or artifact size exceeds budget.
slots.validates: Non-uniform bit allocation beats uniform int6. Opens per-layer export policy as an attack surface.
slots.falsifies: All layers are equally sensitive to quantization. Uniform allocation is already near-optimal.
notes: Cross-domain: Bayesian inference / Fisher information matrix.
Budget-neutral: int4 layers save bytes that offset the int8 layers.

slots.name: sparsify_5pct
slots.broken_invariant: All near-zero weights are kept. Tiny weights quantize to +/-1 and waste compression bandwidth without contributing to model output.
slots.mechanism: Per-tensor: compute magnitude threshold at the sparsify_frac percentile, zero out all weights below it before quantization. Creates long runs of exact zeros that zstd compresses efficiently.
slots.why: Near-zero weights that quantize to +/-1 in int6 contribute almost nothing to model output but consume compression bandwidth. Zeroing them frees bytes for the artifact budget.
slots.early_signal: submission_size_bytes should drop 3-5%. post_quant_bpb should not worsen by more than 0.001.
slots.failure_mode: 5% sparsity degrades quality more than the compression gain is worth. Or zstd already compresses near-zero int6 values well enough.
slots.kill_rule: post_quant_bpb worsens by more than 0.002 vs R0A_export, or artifact shrinks less than 2%.
slots.validates: Pre-quantization sparsification improves compression ratio without killing quality.
slots.falsifies: Even 5% sparsity degrades quality more than the compression gain is worth.

slots.name: companding_plus_sparsify
slots.broken_invariant: One export transform is sufficient. Sparsification and companding attack different parts of the pipeline (zeros for compression, bin spacing for quantization).
slots.mechanism: Apply sparsify_before_quant (zero smallest 5%), then companding (mu-law compress before int6 quantize). Two sequential export transforms.
slots.why: Sparsification creates zeros for better compression. Companding concentrates bins for better quantization of nonzero weights. These are orthogonal improvements that should compose.
slots.early_signal: post_quant_bpb should improve vs R0A_export. Improvement should exceed max(H1, H3) solo if the mechanisms compose.
slots.failure_mode: Combined distortion exceeds compression gain. Or the two transforms interfere (sparsifying changes the distribution that companding expects).
slots.kill_rule: H1H3 < max(H1, H3) — interference, not composition. Or no improvement vs R0A_export.
slots.validates: Two export improvements from different fields compose additively.
slots.falsifies: Over-processing: the combined distortion exceeds the compression gain.
notes: Only interpretable after H1 and H3 solo results.

slots.name: byte_weighted_loss
slots.broken_invariant: One weight per token in CE loss. All tokens contribute equally to training loss regardless of their byte count, but the eval metric (BPB) weights by bytes.
slots.mechanism: Build a byte-weight LUT from the tokenizer. In cross_entropy, use per-token weights = bytes_per_token / mean_bpt. Uses reduction='none' + manual weighted mean.
slots.why: BPB weights tokens by byte count. A 4-byte token matters 4x more for BPB but gets equal CE signal. Weighting CE by bytes/token directly optimizes what's measured.
slots.early_signal: val_bpb should improve relative to R0A at 180s, even if val_loss (unweighted) doesn't change much.
slots.failure_mode: Byte-count distribution is too uniform across tokens to matter. Or reweighting destabilizes optimization by over-emphasizing rare long tokens.
slots.kill_rule: No val_bpb improvement vs R0A at 180s screen, or training loss diverges.
slots.validates: Metric-aligned training improves the metric. Opens loss weighting as an attack surface.
slots.falsifies: The byte-count distribution is too uniform to matter, or the reweighting destabilizes training.
notes: Cross-domain: decision theory / proper scoring rules.
Zero new hyperparameters (weights derived from tokenizer).

slots.name: tapered_mlp_4_3_2
slots.broken_invariant: One MLP width for all layers. Uniform 3x multiplier ignores that information density varies by depth.
slots.mechanism: Per-layer MLP multipliers: 4x for layers 0-3 (early), 3x for layers 4-7 (mid), 2x for layers 8-10 (late). Total params roughly constant. Passed as a list to GPT constructor.
slots.why: Early layers build low-level representations and need capacity. Late layers refine and must quantize well — narrower MLPs have less quantization damage per parameter. Redistributes the same byte budget to where it helps most.
slots.early_signal: pre_quant_bpb should improve at 180s. Also check post_quant_bpb — narrower late layers should quantize better.
slots.failure_mode: Uniform 3x is already well-balanced. Or narrower late layers lose too much quality, offsetting the early-layer gain.
slots.kill_rule: No pre_quant_bpb improvement vs R0A at 180s, or post_quant_bpb worsens.
slots.validates: Non-uniform capacity allocation beats uniform. Opens per-layer architecture as an attack surface.
slots.falsifies: Uniform 3x is already well-balanced, or the narrower late layers lose too much quality.
notes: Total params roughly constant (4+3+3+3+3+3+3+2+2+2+2 = 30 vs 3*11=33 — slightly fewer).

slots.name: quant_anneal
slots.broken_invariant: One QAT regime (binary on/off). Late QAT switches abruptly at 15% LR scale — a discontinuous phase transition in the loss landscape.
slots.mechanism: Replace binary QAT with continuous alpha: w_eff = w + alpha * (w_q - w).detach(). Alpha ramps from 0 at warmdown start to 1 at end. CastedLinear._qat_alpha tracks the interpolation.
slots.why: Abrupt QAT is a quench — the optimizer suddenly sees a different landscape. Gradual annealing lets the optimizer track the continuously deforming landscape, finding better quantized minima.
slots.early_signal: quant_damage (post_quant_bpb - pre_quant_bpb) should shrink vs R0A. The gap reduction should be visible even at 180s.
slots.failure_mode: Optimizer already adapts to abrupt QAT fast enough. Or gradual annealing wastes effective training steps on intermediate alpha values.
slots.kill_rule: No quant_damage improvement vs R0A at 180s, or step_avg_ms worsens materially.
slots.validates: Gradual QAT annealing beats abrupt QAT. Opens the QAT schedule as an attack surface.
slots.falsifies: The optimizer already adapts to QAT fast enough. Gradual annealing just wastes effective training steps.
notes: Replaces late_qat_threshold — the alpha ramp subsumes the binary switch.

slots.name: staged_objective
slots.broken_invariant: One objective for the whole run. Training uses CE throughout with a late binary QAT switch, ignoring that different phases have fundamentally different goals.
slots.mechanism: Three-phase objective: Phase 1 (scale>=0.8): pure CE. Phase 2 (0.3<=scale<0.8): CE + quant-gap KL penalty (penalize divergence between real and quantized outputs). Phase 3 (scale<0.3): CE + quant-gap + deploy alignment. Extra forward pass with quantized weights on micro_step==0 only.
slots.why: Bulk training should focus on representation quality. Pre-warmdown should prepare for quantization. Deep warmdown should fully align with deployed score. Staging the objective gives each phase a specialized engine.
slots.early_signal: quant_damage should shrink vs R0A. post_quant_bpb should improve. Check step_avg_ms for extra overhead from quant-gap forward pass.
slots.failure_mode: Quant-gap penalty hurts representation quality more than it helps deployment. Or extra forward pass costs too many steps, reducing total training.
slots.kill_rule: No post_quant_bpb improvement vs R0A at 180s, or step_avg_ms worse by >15% with no compensating gain.
slots.validates: Multi-phase objectives beat single-objective training. Opens the objective schedule as an attack surface.
slots.falsifies: The quant-gap penalty hurts training quality more than it helps deployment, or the extra forward pass costs too many steps.
notes: CAUTION: extra forward pass for quant-gap costs ~50% more FLOPs on those steps.
Only runs quant-gap forward on micro_step==0 to limit overhead.

slots.name: best_checkpoint_selection
slots.broken_invariant: One checkpoint is always the right export target. The code always exports EMA of the final state, assuming the last step is best.
slots.mechanism: During warmdown (scale < 0.3), every 100 steps: snapshot EMA state, run fast quant-roundtrip eval (quantize->dequantize->eval), track best deployed val_bpb. At export, use the best-deployed checkpoint instead of final EMA.
slots.why: Deployed score = raw quality + quant damage. These trade off non-monotonically during warmdown — raw quality improves while quant damage may worsen. The minimum deployed score may occur at an intermediate step, not the final one.
slots.early_signal: best_ckpt:probe logs should show deployed_bpb variance during warmdown. If the best probe is earlier than the final step, the mechanism is working.
slots.failure_mode: EMA of the final state is already near the deployed-score minimum. Or the periodic quant-eval overhead reduces total training steps enough to offset the selection gain.
slots.kill_rule: No improvement in post_quant_bpb vs R0A at 180s screen, or best_ckpt_step == final_step (selection found nothing better).
slots.validates: Checkpoint selection by deployed metric beats blind last-step export. Opens the export-target decision as an attack surface.
slots.falsifies: EMA of the final state is already near the deployed-score minimum, making selection pointless.
notes: Periodic quant-eval adds overhead. BEST_CKPT_EVERY=100 limits it to ~10-30 probes per warmdown.
